mechanism_narrative: A 5-month increase in industrial production signals a strengthening demand environment that boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 5-day forward returns are not significantly higher than the 5-day moving average in the 6 months following the trigger.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in payroll employment above 200,000 signals a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher than the 20-day moving average in the 6 months following the trigger.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in housing starts below 1.1 million signals a weakening supply environment that reduces new construction and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 60-day forward returns are not significantly lower than the 60-day moving average in the 6 months following the trigger.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in the federal funds rate above 5.5% signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 20-day forward returns are not significantly lower than the 20-day moving average in the 6 months following the trigger.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in real GDP above 4.5% signals a strengthening demand environment that boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 5-day forward returns are not significantly higher than the 5-day moving average in the 6 months following the trigger.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 20-day forward returns are not significantly lower than the 20-day moving average in the 6 months following the trigger.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in real disposable personal income above 4.5% signals a strengthening demand environment that boosts consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher than the 20-day moving average in the 6 months following the trigger.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in the consumer price index above 2.5% signals a strengthening inflation environment that reduces purchasing power and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 60-day forward returns are not significantly lower than the 60-day moving average in the 6 months following the trigger.
